source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - SM Block 281C Platform Well C-11 Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Marsh Island Block 281

actions_taken: Contractors evacuated to Lift Boat New Orleans
Casing beams and casing jack rigged up
Well C-11 tree pulled up 3 inches with casing jack
5,000 psi ball valve installed on 9.625-inch production casing
500-foot cement balance plug set from 11,100 feet to 10,600 feet
Permanent well abandonment completed on 29 September 2023
event.summary: On 9 September 2023, during well abandonment operations on Well C-28 at Apache Corporation's SM Block 281C platform, the 13.375-inch casing on adjacent Well C-11 dropped approximately 3 feet due to severe corrosion failure. The drop sheared off the valve for the 13.375-inch casing completely and partially sheared the valve for the 9.625-inch production casing, releasing 704 psi of gas lift pressure into the atmosphere. The incident was caused by complete loss of the 26-inch conductor casing due to corrosion, which exposed the 13.375-inch surface casing to severe corrosion, ultimately causing it to collapse. Platform grating and decking around Well C-11 were damaged. No injuries or pollution were reported. Apache failed to immediately notify BSEE as required by regulations.
recommendations: Apache Corporation shall submit letter of explanation to BSEE Lafayette District Manager
Apache Corporation shall submit plans to prevent future incidents of this nature
Apache Corporation shall notify BSEE District Office immediately for any incidents involving wellhead falling, structural damage and release of uncontrolled hydrocarbons

threats.name: Corrosion of 26-inch conductor casing leading to complete metal loss
Corrosion of 13.375-inch surface casing due to exposure to oxygen and moisture
Structural failure of 13.375-inch surface casing at collar

consequences.name: Uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons to atmosphere

controls.name: 26-inch conductor casing structural integrity
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to protect inner casings from environmental exposure

supporting_text: The 26-inch conductor casing was severely corroded, and complete metal loss was observed
The complete loss of the 26-inch conductor casing exposed the 13.375-inch surface casing

controls.name: 13.375-inch surface casing structural integrity
controls.barrier_role: Maintain well structural integrity and prevent casing collapse
controls.lod_basis: Secondary containment barrier after conductor casing failure

supporting_text: Years of corrosion caused enough wall loss and metal integrity to part the 13.375-inch surface casing at the collar allowing it to collapse on itself
the 13.375-inch casing on Well C-11 had dropped approximately 3 feet
active corrosion to begin due to exposure to oxygen and moisture

controls.name: B-Annulus casing valve on 13.375-inch surface casing
controls.barrier_role: Isolate annular pressure and prevent release
controls.lod_basis: Active isolation device to control annular pressure

supporting_text: the B-Annulus casing valve attached to the wellhead for the 13.375-inch surface casing had been fully sheared off

controls.name: A-Annulus casing valve on 9.625-inch production casing
controls.barrier_role: Isolate production casing pressure and prevent gas release
controls.lod_basis: Active isolation device to control production casing pressure

supporting_text: the valve for the 9.625-inch production casing was partially sheared off leaking gas from the well
The A-Annulus casing valve for the 9.625-inch production casing was partially sheared off from the wellhead and gas lift pressure was released into the atmosphere
704 psi of gas lift pressure was released into the atmosphere from the 9.625-inch production casing

controls.name: Monthly casing inspection program
controls.lod_basis: Proactive monitoring and inspection to identify degradation

supporting_text: BSEE reviewed the last monthly casing report for Well C-11 conducted on 21 August 2023, that stated the A-Annulus current pressure was 0 psi

controls.name: Stop Work Authority procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent escalation by stopping operations when hazard identified
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to halt operations and assess situation

supporting_text: The Co-Rep called a Stop Work Authority with all personnel on the lift boat

controls.name: Emergency evacuation procedure
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from harm during incident
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to remove personnel from danger

supporting_text: the well abandonment contractors evacuated to the Lift Boat New Orleans
The contract crew evacuated from the production platform onto the Lift Boat New Orleans

controls.name: Emergency well control response
controls.lod_basis: Recovery actions to regain control after loss of containment

supporting_text: The B&B Technician installed a 5,000 pounds per square inch (psi) ball valve on the 9.625-inch production casing
Then a 500-foot cement balance plug was set from 11,100 feet to 10,600 feet consisting of 189 cubic feet of cement securing the well

controls.name: Incident notification procedure to BSEE
controls.barrier_role: Ensure regulatory oversight and appropriate response
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory communication requirement for incident management

supporting_text: Apache failed to notify BSEE as per 30CFR 250.188 and NTL 2019-NO5 of an incident that resulted in structural damage to the platform, safety systems, and causing an uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons into the atmosphere
Apache Corporation failed to immediately notify the BSEE Lafayette District Manager of an incident that occurred on 9 September 2023

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
